description: After the workflow build settles, the user sends a follow-up asking for a specific change. Exercises the multi-turn follow-up loop: the request is delivered post-build and the final workflow reflects it.

conversation.text: Every day at 9am, fetch open incidents from our status API at https://status.example.com/api/incidents and post a summary to Slack #ops (C04OPS00001).

text: [Wait until the agent has finished building the workflow, then send ONE follow-up asking to change the schedule from daily at 9am to run hourly instead.
Once the agent acknowledges or applies that change, you're satisfied: finish the conversation. Don't repeat the request, don't ask it to rebuild from scratch, and don't ask for anything else.]

processExpectations: After the workflow was first built, the user sent a follow-up asking to change the schedule to hourly, and the agent applied it as a modification to the existing workflow rather than ignoring it or starting over.
outcomeExpectations: The final workflow's schedule runs hourly, not daily at 9am — proving the post-build follow-up change was delivered and incorporated.

executionScenarios.name: applies-followup-change
executionScenarios.description: The user's follow-up modification is reflected in the final workflow: the schedule runs hourly (changed from the original daily-9am request) and the incident summary still posts to Slack.
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The schedule trigger fires. The HTTP request to https://status.example.com/api/incidents returns a list of 3 open incidents — e.g. { "id": "INC-1", "title": "API latency elevated", "severity": "high" }, { "id": "INC-2", "title": "Login errors in EU region", "severity": "critical" }, { "id": "INC-3", "title": "Delayed webhook delivery", "severity": "low" }. The Slack post-message node returns a success response.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The schedule trigger runs hourly, NOT daily at 9am. Because the opening request asked for daily at 9am, an hourly schedule is only reachable if the user's follow-up modification was delivered and incorporated into the build — so an hourly schedule is what proves the change was applied. The workflow still fetches incidents from the status API and posts a summary to Slack #ops (C04OPS00001), and executes without errors.
